sourceNotes: Haryana: Om Prakash Chautala CM (2001-2005), then Bhupinder Singh Hooda CM (2005 onwards). Main issues: SYL canal water disputes, Kandela farmers agitations, Gurgaon IT/SEZ industrializations, Honda factory worker strikes. Built programmatically matching the schema and calibration constraints.

newsItems.title: Chautala Demands Early SYL Canal Construction (2001-01)
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Om Prakash Chautala demands the immediate completion of the Satluj Yamuna Link (SYL) canal, accusing the Punjab government of deliberately denying Haryana its share of water.

reactionOptions.text: The state government defends the demand as essential to secure water for Haryana's dry southern districts, addressing the syl canal issues.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in central project clearances spark localized protests from border farmer groups in relation to syl canal.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition Congress leaders claim Chautala is politicizing the canal issue to cover up domestic failures, criticizing the handling of the syl canal developments.

risk.badOutcome: The Congress criticism is ignored as rural voter groups rally behind the state's water claims regarding the syl canal.

reactionOptions.text: A joint committee of water engineers and legal experts is formed to represent Haryana's case at the center, seeking a consensus on the syl canal situation.

risk.badOutcome: Liaison discussions are delayed by a lack of coordination in the central water commission in the syl canal joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The Water Resources Secretary declines to comment on the projected cost changes of the unfinished SYL canal, citing the sensitivity of the syl canal context.

newsItems.title: Gurgaon Industrial Land Allocation Scheme Approved (2001-02)
newsItems.description: The Haryana government approves massive infrastructure allocations and land concessions for new industrial parks in Gurgaon, aiming to draw manufacturing and IT investments.

reactionOptions.text: The Industries Department launches special verification camps to allot plots in Gurgaon, addressing the gurgaon plots issues.

risk.badOutcome: Missing land records delay the allotments, drawing protests from industrial buyers in relation to gurgaon plots.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition leaders claim the land allocation rules favor select industrial developers, criticizing the handling of the gurgaon plots developments.

risk.badOutcome: The developer allegations fail to gain traction as urban youth welcome local employment options regarding the gurgaon plots.

reactionOptions.text: A joint committee of HUDA officials and corporate representatives is formed to monitor Gurgaon layout plans, seeking a consensus on the gurgaon plots situation.

risk.badOutcome: Liaison discussions are delayed by a lack of coordination in the municipal development board in the gurgaon plots joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The Industry Secretary declines to comment on the specific valuation metrics used for Gurgaon lands, citing the sensitivity of the gurgaon plots context.

newsItems.title: Primary School English Medium Reforms (2001-03)
newsItems.description: The state enforces English instruction starting from Class 1 in primary schools, drawing criticism from local language advocates.

reactionOptions.text: The Education Department defends the early English policy as essential to prepare rural students for modern IT careers, addressing the 2001 03 issues.

risk.badOutcome: Disputes over specific block project allocations stall municipal council meetings in relation to 2001 03.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition leaders demand that primary education remain strictly in regional languages to protect local identity, criticizing the handling of the 2001 03 developments.

risk.badOutcome: The opposition claims are labeled as exaggerated by political observers, limiting their public impact regarding the 2001 03.

reactionOptions.text: A joint committee of educators and parents is formed to draft regional language manuals, seeking a consensus on the 2001 03 situation.

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over the tax sharing formula stall the municipal finance reforms in the 2001 03 joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The Education Secretary declines to comment on the number of primary schools served with instructions, citing the sensitivity of the 2001 03 context.

reactionOptions.text: The Health Department deploys mobile medical camps and sends emergency drug consignments to Rohtak, addressing the 2003 07 issues.

risk.badOutcome: Disputes over specific block project allocations stall municipal council meetings in relation to 2003 07.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition BJP leaders visit the affected blocks, accusing the government of ignoring primary health centers, criticizing the handling of the 2003 07 developments.

risk.badOutcome: The opposition claims are labeled as exaggerated by political observers, limiting their public impact regarding the 2003 07.

reactionOptions.text: A joint legislative-medical panel is formed to review municipal healthcare infrastructure and drug supply chains, seeking a consensus on the 2003 07 situation.

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over the tax sharing formula stall the municipal finance reforms in the 2003 07 joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The Health Directorate declines to release the official casualty figures from the Rohtak outbreak, citing the sensitivity of the 2003 07 context.

newsItems.title: Ghaggar River Flooding (2003-08)

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over the tax sharing formula stall the municipal finance reforms in the 2003 08 joint efforts.
